what_it_does: Implements a full-viewport modal settings surface for a React application, organizing configuration into six navigable sections (Brain & persona, Modes, Voice, Model roles, Composition, Cognition) and delegating all reads and writes to existing controller handlers and endpoints exposed through AppContext.
observations: Full-viewport modal scrim/panel with a left rail of icon+name sections and a content pane.
Six section subcomponents: BrainSection, ModesSection, VoiceSection, RolesSection, CompositionSection, CognitionSection.
Brain section shows a resource-fit bar, model picker, temperature, max tokens, context-window restart, endpoint, timeout, and a persona grid.
Modes section renders the mode registry with directive, resolution details, sub-types, consent style, and an auto-detect toggle.
Voice section controls voice on/off, listening style, STT ear status + start, TTS engine selection, per-engine knobs, and voice path.
Roles section binds models to named model-function roles via setCfgSlot.
Composition section is read-only, surfacing R2_ context-ranking weights.
Cognition section lets the operator author a role, run a role, and view the run index.
Heavy inline comments cite merge-plan sections, reuse rules, and design principles.
Uses shared kit components (SectionHead, Badge, Surface, EmptyState) and data-ui-ref UI addresses.
Most inputs use defaultValue + onBlur rather than fully controlled state.
standouts: Cognition section surfaces agent-style role authoring and execution directly inside the settings modal.
Resource-fit visualization is placed at the top of the Brain section.
Mode registry is rendered as a hierarchy that includes context-resolution declarations.
Explicit architectural stance: reuse existing handlers/endpoints, no new write paths, registry-is-truth.
conventions: Functional React components with props typed as `any`.
Reads global state and acts through a custom `useApp` context hook.
Uses shared kit components and CSS classes prefixed with `set-` and `settings-`.
Inline documentation comments above each component/section.
UI test addresses attached via `data-ui-ref` (or `dataUiRef` prop on kit components).
Local `useState` reserved for draft form values; persisted values live in AppContext.
concerns: Widespread `any` typing on component props and callback arguments reduces type safety.
Long architectural comments may drift from the actual code over time.
`dataUiRef` prop assumes the kit Surface maps it to a `data-ui-ref` attribute; not verifiable here.
Model index selection matches on `base_url`, which may fail when the row omits it.
FitBar renders a Surface even when no GPU items are present, leaving an empty bar.
Cognition create form intentionally omits richer spec fields; the boundary is documented but easy to overshoot.
notes: This is the consolidated settings modal that folds brain, voice, mode, role, composition, and cognition controls into one navigable surface while reusing existing AppContext handlers.
questions: Where are the CSS/token definitions for classes like `settings-scrim`, `fit-seg`, and `set-pill`?
Does the `Surface` kit component correctly translate a `dataUiRef` prop into `data-ui-ref`?
What is the exact accepted shape of the spec passed to `createCogRole`?
How does `setCfgSlot` deep-merge nested objects such as `roles`?
Are `chatModelsX` rows guaranteed to include `service`, `up`, and `base_url` fields?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — the file is a consolidated, full-viewport settings modal with the six advertised sections and reuses existing endpoints/handlers rather than adding new ones.
intention_signals: Build a single, coherent operator control surface that exposes model, voice, mode, role, composition, and cognition configuration without duplicating state or creating new backend endpoints, using a shared design kit and explicit UI addresses.
novelty: The Cognition section brings agent-style role authoring and run-index visibility directly into the settings surface, and the file is densely annotated with merge-plan/architectural rationale.
contribution: A unified, sectioned settings modal component with reusable section subcomponents and a pattern of delegating all configuration mutations to existing AppContext handlers, plus an embedded human-facing cognition control surface.
summary_for_embedding: React settings modal component exposing brain, mode, voice, role, composition, and cognition configuration through a left-rail navigable full-screen dialog.
intention_for_embedding: To consolidate scattered configuration controls into one coherent operator surface that reuses existing backend handlers and registry reads.
suggested_fields: reuse_contract: existing handler/endpoint used by each write
ui_address_carriers: DOM elements carrying data-ui-ref addresses

symbols.does: Renders the full-viewport settings modal and coordinates which section is active.
symbols.description: Uses useApp to read settings visibility, current tab, registries, and handlers. It renders a scrim/panel layout with a left navigation rail and switches between Brain, Modes, Voice, Roles, Composition, and Cognition sections. It deliberately avoids adding a second ui://settings carrier because the Toolbar button already carries that address.

symbols.does: Visualizes GPU memory fit for selected brain and voice models.
symbols.description: Receives a fitReport, computes segment widths as percentages of a ceiling, and renders a segmented bar inside a kit Surface with tone set to sig or fail. It also explains why the selection won't fit and suggests evictions when applicable.

symbols.does: Converts megabytes to a percentage of the fit ceiling.
symbols.description: Clamps the result at 100 and is used to size visual bar segments.

symbols.does: Converts megabytes to gigabytes formatted to one decimal.
symbols.description: Used in fit labels and tooltips.

symbols.does: Renders brain model, knobs, endpoint, timeout, and persona selection.
symbols.description: Provides a model dropdown backed by chatModelsX with load-on-pick, temperature and max-token inputs, context-window restart for local vLLM rows, endpoint and timeout fields, and a persona grid. It includes the FitBar at the top.

symbols.does: Renders the mode registry and auto-detect toggle.
symbols.description: Displays top-level modes with directive, context resolution details, sub-types, consent style, and an active badge. It lets the operator switch modes and set the MODE_AUTODETECT slot via setCfgSlot.

symbols.does: Renders voice on/off, listening style, STT ear, TTS engines, knobs, and voice path.
symbols.description: Controls voice_enabled, push-to-talk vs auto-listen, STT selection and ear start, TTS engine selection, optional voice argument, per-engine knob display, and selectable voice paths.
Lists roles from the registry, shows bound/unbound state, and provides a select to bind a model via setCfgSlot('roles', ...).

symbols.does: Read-only display of R2 context-ranking configuration.
symbols.description: Filters keys starting with R2_ from compositionCfg and renders each as a kit Surface card.

symbols.does: Provides human-facing role authoring, role execution, and run-index viewing.
symbols.description: Maintains local form state for creating a role and running a role, calls createCogRole and runCogRole from AppContext, and renders the run list with refresh. It deliberately limits the create spec to known ROLE_FIELDS keys and flags deeper authoring as out of scope.

symbols.does: Builds and submits a new cognition role spec.
symbols.description: Constructs an object with id, label, op, input_addresses, and optional prompt_template, then calls createCogRole. It intentionally omits model binding and advanced input/output schema wiring because those are flagged as deeper passes.
